que: What do Kotlin extensions allow you to do?
options: Add callable functionality to existing types without modifying them
Change the JVM bytecode of a class at runtime
Store hidden fields in third-party classes

que: Which are the two common forms of Kotlin extensions?

options: When working with third-party types you cannot modify

que: What is a good extension practice?
options: Check whether the standard library already provides what you need
Hide important business rules in random extension files
Assume all extensions are dynamic dispatch

que: What is a common extension mistake?
options: Assuming they truly modify the target class
